instructions.instruction: ALWAYS prefer defuddle over WebFetch for standard web pages such as articles, blog posts, and documentation pages.
instructions.relevant_when: User provides a URL to a standard publicly accessible web page and asks the agent to read, summarize, or analyze its content.

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS use the --md flag when running defuddle parse to receive markdown output instead of raw HTML.
instructions.relevant_when: Any time defuddle parse is invoked and the output will be read or processed by the agent or user.

instructions.instruction: Use the -o flag to save defuddle output to a file; NEVER use shell redirection (>) to write output.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks the agent to save or persist the extracted content to a local file.

instructions.instruction: Use -p <field> (e.g. -p title, -p description) only when the user explicitly needs metadata, not full page content.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks for the title, description, or domain of a page rather than its full text.

instructions.instruction: Fall back to WebFetch or inform the user when the target URL is login-gated or requires authentication, because defuddle fetches without credentials.
instructions.relevant_when: User provides a URL that is behind a login wall, authentication redirect, or requires session cookies.

instructions.instruction: Fall back to WebFetch or inform the user when the page is a JavaScript-heavy SPA whose content is rendered client-side, because defuddle cannot execute JavaScript.
instructions.relevant_when: User provides a URL that is known to be a React/Vue/Angular SPA where content is injected by JavaScript after page load.

instructions.instruction: If defuddle output is empty or contains only navigation text, fall back to WebFetch and inform the user.
instructions.relevant_when: defuddle parse returns an empty body or content that looks like a site-nav skeleton rather than article prose.
